step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two main user personas: the instructor (UserCompanyAgent) and the student (UserConsumerWeb). The infrastructure is provisioned via CloudFormation, which creates RDS, FSx, and triggers a Lambda custom resource. Lambda invokes Systems Manager to configure an EC2 instance that modifies the AppStream image builder script, signaling back to CloudFormation. Students connect to the AppStream instance, which talks to the license server on EC2, mounts FSx, and queries RDS.

graph.name: IHS Markit - Automated AppStream Provisioning
graph.notes: Automated setup of high-end GPU training software delivery using AppStream 2.0 with RDS SQL Express and FSx.

nodes.name: CloudFormation
nodes.notes: Orchestrates creation of RDS, FSx, and AppStream resources.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggered by Custom Resource to initiate configuration.

nodes.name: Systems Manager
nodes.notes: Executes remote configuration command on EC2.

nodes.name: EC2 / License Server
nodes.notes: Acts as the license server and executes the session-mount configuration scripts.

nodes.name: AppStream 2.0
nodes.notes: Delivers petrotechnical software to students via browser streams.

nodes.name: FSx
nodes.notes: Stores project files, dynamically mounted to student sessions.

nodes.name: RDS SQL Express
nodes.notes: Low-cost SQL Express database for training session metadata.

nodes.name: Student
nodes.notes: Accesses the software training environment via web browser.
